entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer that tracks user, session, and agent states across platforms. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval system and temporal reasoning to ensure agents recall past events and future intentions with time-aware accuracy.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'brain' for Hermes Agent features a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts entities without requiring additional LLM calls. It maintains long-term coherence through automated cron-driven consolidation cycles and a hybrid search architecture combining vector, BM25, and graph methods.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a sophisticated 'retain, recall, and reflect' workflow that allows agents to learn from their experiences over time. It is designed for enterprise-grade reliability, offering two-line code integration and support for robust storage backends like PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library that uses asynchronous reasoning to extract conclusions from conversation history and track evolving relationships between users and agents. It serves as the foundational upstream library for several Hermes-specific memory implementations, providing prompt-ready context via dedicated SDKs.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a five-role architecture to help agents manage strategy, analysis, and curation for complex tasks. It focuses on iterative self-improvement, producing reusable playbooks and detailed execution traces that persist knowledge across agent runs.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A specialized plugin for Hermes Agent that utilizes a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) to ensure no message is ever lost during context compaction. It provides agents with tools to search and expand compressed history stored in a persistent SQLite-backed message store.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for the Honcho memory system, enabling private cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent users. It includes an automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis, supporting any OpenAI-compatible or local inference provider.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first memory engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It integrates via MCP servers and agent plugins, using a combination of BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs to manage context privately.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: ContextPilot optimizes long-context inference by reordering and deduplicating shared context blocks to reduce input token overhead. It functions as a native plugin for Hermes Agent, boosting prefill throughput by up to 3x across various inference engines like vLLM and llama.cpp.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format, making it ideal for multi-agent systems with zero API costs. It employs ACT-R activation logic to strengthen relevant facts while allowing stale information to decay naturally over time.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases, documents, and media with automatic summarization and OCR capabilities. It uses bidirectional edge tags to create a navigable relationship network, accessible to agents through MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency memory system for Hermes Agents that achieves sub-millisecond performance using SQLite-backed storage. It features native vector and full-text search without requiring external API keys, ensuring a lightweight and private integration.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: This memory kernel stack consolidates temporal continuity, graph-based truth, and corpus retrieval into a single 'five-shelf' architecture. It prevents flat memory blobs by organizing data through a native Hermes-Agent plugin powered by SQLite and Kuzu.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this anticipatory memory system prefetches context by watching agent session logs in real-time. It is a local-first, MCP-native server that utilizes SQLite-vec and GGUF models to provide low-latency RAG capabilities.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: This utility reduces the token overhead of Hermes Agent tool schemas by using BM25 ranking to select only the most relevant keywords. It includes a dashboard for auditing token savings and supports both Anthropic Tool Search and local provider fallbacks.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This project provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major industry frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes to perform sub-second AI skill discovery across 26 specialized security domains using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw automates the evolution of skill libraries by deduplicating and improving tools based on interaction history. It natively supports Hermes, allowing for a unified skill ecosystem that scales across multiple agents and user groups.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This auditing tool helps Hermes eliminate detectable AI writing patterns by using a tiered word replacement table and a two-pass audit system. It detects 42 distinct patterns to ensure content remains precise and avoids common inflation or recycled transitions.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that offers modular installation of skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It is fully compatible with agentskills.io, making it a versatile foundation for expanding an agent's professional capabilities.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This implementation of the Agent Skills open specification brings type-safe skill support to Pydantic AI. It utilizes progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token usage while supporting both filesystem and programmatic skill definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions with built-in layout auto-fixing. It supports six diagram presets and can export results to multiple formats including PNG, SVG, and PDF.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes empowers the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It identifies structural conservation laws in codebases and generates constraint reports to highlight analysis blind spots.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library enables Hermes to interact with Chainlink oracle networks for smart contract operations like CCIP and Data Feeds. It follows the agentskills.io specification to provide secure, standardized blockchain capabilities to the agent.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave prose, diagrams, and source code into unified .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between documentation and source files.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides a shared memory system via markdown-based directories, allowing Hermes to remember work across different instances. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality data to fine-tune replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry for reusable AI skills that includes a Python SDK and CLI for lifecycle management. It uniquely supports skill monetization through Stripe and the TON blockchain while handling automated dependency resolution.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official FLUX skills provide Hermes with standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for high-quality image generation. The skill supports precise hex color specification and structured JSON prompting for the FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 model families.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing Hermes to expand its own capabilities through slash commands.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a long-running agent harness that uses on-disk state management instead of volatile chat history for structured workflows. It features a plan-approve-execute cycle and passive handoff envelopes for seamless task resumption.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It provides real-time tracking of decisions and progress, ensuring safe rollbacks if a long-running task encounters an error.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize context token usage for Hermes. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading for dynamic configuration.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts repositories and documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures high reliability by providing verifiable audit trails with file-and-line level provenance for every generated skill.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: Hermeshub serves as a community marketplace for browsing and installing Hermes skills with one-click simplicity. It incorporates automated security scanning and an agent-to-agent feedback system to ensure the reliability and safety of shared skills.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# implementation of the agentskills.io specification allows .NET developers to integrate Hermes skills using Microsoft.Extensions.AI. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files and automatic registration of skill directories.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill grants Hermes the ability to manage files, calendars, and contacts through Nextcloud using standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It includes a guided setup script to ensure secure communication via HTTPS and App Passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a comprehensive collection of Nix packages for AI coding agents, including Hermes, with automated daily updates to ensure access to the latest versions. It is ideal for developers seeking a reproducible, Nix-based environment that includes orchestrators, CLI agents, and unified API proxies.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security deployments by utilizing NVIDIA OpenShell for hardware-level sandboxing and kernel-level enforcement. It offers multi-layer protection against unauthorized network egress and filesystem writes through pre-configured, strict security policy presets.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services with a focus on secure secrets handling via sops-nix. It supports a hybrid skill architecture, allowing for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI tool installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and supports local model execution via LM Studio. It features a GUI, a dynamic tool maker, and portable AI generation servers, eliminating the need for Docker or complex installation processes.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes mini-swe-agent and the official Hermes installation. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using flexible build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances with distinct identities and workspaces from a single web application. It is tailored for complex operations, featuring hierarchy-aware delegation and enterprise MCP access for external integrations.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is optimized for headless operation and the autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs via Nous Portal integration.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option provides a Docker Compose stack integrated with a ttyd web terminal for browser-based agent interaction. It is designed to be deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack, utilizing GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages complex multi-agent workflows using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It serves as a central hub for Hermes by integrating major frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen while enforcing human-in-the-loop oversight through its Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms that support over 23 LLM providers, including native Hermes and local Ollama integrations. It distinguishes itself with durable memory systems and a unique 'conversation-to-skill' learning mechanism that allows agents to acquire new capabilities through reviewed interactions.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: Focused on performance optimization, this project uses an automated outer-loop search process to refine the harness code used by Hermes Agent. It orchestrates benchmark evaluations across TBLite and TB2, utilizing structured candidate mutation to push the performance frontiers of the model.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: This competitive arena orchestrates two rival AI companies, each powered by nine autonomous agents, in a race to gain GitHub stars. By leveraging Hermes Agent for task execution and learning, it provides a real-time dashboard to visualize agent-vs-agent competition and scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates a massive pipeline of 17 specialized agents covering everything from research to infrastructure. It ensures high-quality code output by enforcing mandatory reviewer and tester gates while utilizing MCP servers for external web fetching and documentation access.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase process that enforces architectural integrity through automated graph-diff gates at every stage of development.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation tool routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code using an ACP-style workflow. It is designed to prevent context pollution in parent agents by isolating delegation tasks and enforcing safety through strict timeouts and output limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This high-performance Rust TUI tracks token consumption and associated costs across various AI coding assistants, including Hermes Agent. It provides developers with local, real-time monitoring of usage metrics for multiple models and integrations within a single terminal interface.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based configuration files for banners, spinners, and ASCII art. Themes can inherit from default settings, enabling partial skin definitions that are easy to create and share with the community.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically includes specialized MLOps tools for managing vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training workflows.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Designed for autonomous agent control, this AI-native browser enables Hermes Agent to navigate the web via native MCP integration. It features a human-in-the-loop supervisory UI for live approvals and automatically infers page schemas to assist agents with structured data detection.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that operate with project-specific memories, tools, and instructions. It allows for per-folder configuration, letting developers seamlessly switch between global settings and localized agent behaviors based on their current workspace.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static analysis tool uses regex and AST to detect failure modes like ambiguity or missing constraints in agent configurations and system prompts. It provides deterministic scoring without requiring LLM calls, supporting various formats including YAML, JSON, and .prompt files.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility facilitates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of complex model configurations and credentials from local environment files to ensure a smooth setup.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script provides a zero-cost entry point to the Hermes ecosystem by bundling the agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker. It automates the configuration of 29 community plugins and sets up local GPU inference for immediate use.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This interface provides a streamlined three-panel layout that mirrors the full feature set of the Hermes CLI for web and mobile users. It distinguishes itself with a visual context ring for real-time token monitoring and an integrated file browser for direct workspace interaction.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-channel connectivity, allowing users to configure and manage Hermes across platforms like Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It features robust session management and detailed usage analytics to track token consumption and estimated costs across different models.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace integrates a terminal, memory browser, and skill manager into a single interface designed for zero-fork compatibility with vanilla installations. It is unique for its Swarm Mode, which enables autonomous multi-agent orchestration and role-based dispatching.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway that acts as a command center for starting, stopping, and configuring individual agent profiles. The UI features collapsible tool-call cards and a suite of system metrics to monitor the health and performance of the underlying AI stack.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: Built specifically for macOS, this desktop client establishes direct SSH connections to remote Hermes hosts without requiring intermediary APIs. It provides a native experience for managing remote files and sessions, supplemented by a Kanban board for task organization.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React application brings a terminal-style chat experience directly into the Telegram ecosystem as a Mini App. It supports background agent spawning and live output monitoring, complete with system health gauges and cron job management.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows solution that runs without admin rights or Docker, offering a GUI for local models via LM Studio. It stands out with over 100 built-in tools—including TTS and ComfyUI—and a dynamic tool maker for creating custom Python handlers.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This companion app simplifies the setup process with a guided installer for both local instances and remote API connections. It excels in integration, supporting 16 different messaging gateways and providing built-in management for 14 distinct toolsets and memory systems.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a read-only view of configuration files, environment variables, and cron jobs. It features FTS5-powered search for deep inspection of conversation histories and real-time tracking of gateway status and costs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: This self-hosted workspace emphasizes modularity through an integrated marketplace for discovering and installing skills and MCP servers. It utilizes isolated profiles to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate while providing real-time tool approval cards in the chat.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Featuring a modern glassmorphic design, this single-file React interface offers real-time log streaming and inline workspace previews. It provides a lightweight yet capable environment for managing MCP tools, skills, and scheduled agent tasks.
